title: A happier Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: A happier Evangelion.             Quote Post by Adamski » Sat Jun 19, 2010 1:59 am After watching Rebuild 1.0 and 2.22, it's hard not to notice that the themes throughout the show are a lot less depressing than the original. The characters seem less withdrawn than before as they practice quite a bit more disclosure with one another. This seems almost necessary as the original show wasn't very clear on these things, leaving simple things such as character motivation up to the audience to decide (Resulting only in heated debates over the internet). Something leads me to believe this reflects the attitude of the creators, specifically Anno. I remember hearing that he was suffering from some severe depression during the production of Evangelion, and I wonder if that since he is now officially richer than god, his happier outlook is being projected onto the characters of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat Jun 19, 2010 4:23 am Well, keep in mind that if you only watched the first half of Eva T.V., you would have wondered what the big deal was, too. It wasn't 'til we got to the climax that the real wrist slitting began. There's still plenty of time, yet... in Rebuild, maybe Pen Pen will be the one torn apart by harpies (OMG spoilaz!)

[]:             Quote Post by Adamski » Sat Jun 19, 2010 12:20 pm Pen Pen is literally the best character in the show. If Anno manages to kill him (her?) off, he'd easily get twice as many death threats than he did for the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Sat Jun 19, 2010 12:35 pm That would mean... two death threats?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Sat Jun 19, 2010 2:21 pm Jinroh wrote: That would mean... two death threats?

[]: Re: A happier Evangelion.             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Sat Jun 19, 2010 5:43 pm Adamski wrote: After watching Rebuild 1.0 and 2.22, it's hard not to notice that the themes throughout the show are a lot less depressing than the original. The characters seem less withdrawn than before as they practice quite a bit more disclosure with one another. This seems almost necessary as the original show wasn't very clear on these things, leaving simple things such as character motivation up to the audience to decide (Resulting only in heated debates over the internet). Something leads me to believe this reflects the attitude of the creators, specifically Anno. I remember hearing that he was suffering from some severe depression during the production of Evangelion, and I wonder if that since he is now officially richer than god, his happier outlook is being projected onto the characters of the show.

[]: Re: A happier Evangelion.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Sat Jun 19, 2010 7:27 pm The Fourth Evangelion wrote: It's also because it appearently needs to be a version of the story that is also understandable for casual anime-fans and not just us Diehards

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jun 19, 2010 7:32 pm I remember how gloomy 1.0 was compared to the series' equivalent. In fact, it was noted for being significantly so, and with less fanservice. So I think, at this point, 2.0's upbeatness is an aberration. However, I do think that the message of Rebuild as a whole is going to be more upbeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jun 19, 2010 8:15 pm > A happier Evangelion God, I hope not.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Mon Jun 21, 2010 10:30 am It's already pretty damn dark. End of Evangelion is a happy ending, really. Even the TV show had a super happy ending with everyone saying "Congratulations". Might not have been the ending people wanted but it was still not a tragedy by any stretch of the imagination. Some things that happen in RoE 2 are just brutal and much more emotionally damaging. 1.11 and the first half of 2 seemed upbeat, but so did the first half of the original. We have two movies left and I don't think it's going to be all flowers and puppy dogs.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 21, 2010 10:37 am Monk Ed wrote: I remember how gloomy 1.0 was compared to the series' equivalent. In fact, it was noted for being significantly so, and with less fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Jun 21, 2010 10:51 am I don't think the movies are really any happier; the characters are just painted in broader strokes to fit the film format. Some of them actually seem sicker in some ways: Shinji seems a lot more obsessed with his father (almost to the point of caricature, I'd argue), while Asuka seems more socially retarded than she was in the TV show.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Mon Jun 21, 2010 12:51 pm Lucretius wrote: while Asuka seems more socially retarded than she was in the TV show.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jun 21, 2010 2:05 pm Lucretius wrote: Shinji seems a lot more obsessed with his father (almost to the point of caricature, I'd argue)

[]:             Quote Post by Mishrak » Tue Jun 22, 2010 10:51 am The aquarium scene was a nice reprieve from an otherwise extremely gloomy atmosphere. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Tue Jun 22, 2010 10:54 am Eva's only true happy fluffy bunny scene IMO is "CONGRATULATION'S"! 'Yuck'!

[]:             Quote Post by duexmachina » Wed Jun 23, 2010 3:59 am ^ episode 9 was pretty happy.

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Wed Jun 23, 2010 3:48 pm Possibly.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Wed Jun 23, 2010 4:06 pm Fallingtogrey wrote: Uhm, am I the only one that finds it odd that Misato says "Rei wants to be CUPID's arrow for Shinji and his father" when she's talking to Asuka about the dinner at Rei's?. I swear Misato actually says Cupid too, it's not a translation error.
